things.name: Tallinn Old Town (Vanalinn)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage perfectly preserved medieval city — one of the best in northern Europe

things.name: Toompea Castle & Alexander Nevsky Cathedral
things.description: Upper town with the Estonian Parliament building and the onion-domed Russian Orthodox cathedral

things.name: Town Hall Square (Raekoja Plats)
things.description: Medieval heart of the city with the 15th-century Town Hall and surrounding merchants' guild houses

things.name: Kumu Art Museum
things.description: Estonia's largest art museum in a dramatic limestone building with Soviet-era and contemporary Estonian art

things.name: Kadriorg Palace & Park
things.description: Peter the Great's Baroque palace in a beautiful park with rose gardens and swans

things.name: Patarei Prison Sea Fortress
things.description: Eerie Soviet-era prison on the waterfront — preserved in a state of decay as a memorial to totalitarianism

things.name: Telliskivi Creative City
things.description: Trendy repurposed factory complex with flea markets, restaurants, street art and creative studios

things.name: Viru Gate & Medieval Wall Towers
things.description: Tallinn's most photographed medieval gate towers — walk along surviving sections of the 14th-century walls

things.name: Estonian Open Air Museum
things.description: Huge rural heritage museum with 72 historic farmsteads, windmills and cottages from the 18th-20th centuries

things.name: Helsinki Day Trip by Ferry
things.description: 2-hour ferry across the Gulf of Finland to Helsinki — a popular day trip for both cities' visitors
